kmsKeyId.description: The ID of the KMS key that is used for encryption.

encryptionType.description: The type of encryption. Set to KMS_BASED_ENCRYPTION to use a KMS key that you own and manage. Set to FLEETWISE_DEFAULT_ENCRYPTION to use an Amazon Web Services managed key that is owned by the Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise service account.

errorMessage.description: The error message that describes why encryption settings couldn't be configured, if applicable.

creationTime.description: The time when encryption was configured in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time when encryption was last updated in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

cloudWatchLogDelivery.description: Returns information about log delivery to Amazon CloudWatch Logs.

logType.description: The type of log to send data to Amazon CloudWatch Logs.

logGroupName.description: The Amazon CloudWatch Logs group the operation sends data to.

customerAccountId.description: The unique ID of the Amazon Web Services account, provided at account creation.

accountStatus.description:
The status of registering your account and resources. The status can be one of:

  • REGISTRATION_SUCCESS - The Amazon Web Services resource is successfully registered.

  • REGISTRATION_PENDING - Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise is processing the registration request. This process takes approximately five minutes to complete.

  • REGISTRATION_FAILURE - Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise can't register the AWS resource. Try again later.

timestreamRegistrationResponse.description: Information about the registered Amazon Timestream resources or errors, if any.

iamRegistrationResponse.description: Information about the registered IAM resources or errors, if any.

creationTime.description: The time the account was registered, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time this registration was last updated, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

timestreamTableName.description: The name of the Timestream database table.

timestreamDatabaseArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Timestream database.

timestreamTableArn.description: The ARN of the Timestream database table.

registrationStatus.description: The status of registering your Amazon Timestream resources. The status can be one of REGISTRATION_SUCCESS, REGISTRATION_PENDING, REGISTRATION_FAILURE.

errorMessage.description: A message associated with a registration error.

campaignSummaries.description: A summary of information about each campaign.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of a campaign.

description.description: The description of the campaign.

signalCatalogArn.description: The ARN of the signal catalog associated with the campaign.

targetArn.description: The ARN of a vehicle or fleet to which the campaign is deployed.

status.description:
The state of a campaign. The status can be one of the following:

  • CREATING - Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise is processing your request to create the campaign.

  • WAITING_FOR_APPROVAL - After a campaign is created, it enters the WAITING_FOR_APPROVAL state. To allow Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise to deploy the campaign to the target vehicle or fleet, use the API operation to approve the campaign.

  • RUNNING - The campaign is active.

  • SUSPENDED - The campaign is suspended. To resume the campaign, use the API operation.

lastModificationTime.description: The last time the campaign was modified.

summaries.description: A list of information about each decoder manifest.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

arn.description: The ARN of a vehicle model (model manifest) associated with the decoder manifest.

modelManifestArn.description: The ARN of a vehicle model (model manifest) associated with the decoder manifest.

description.description: A brief description of the decoder manifest.

status.description: The state of the decoder manifest. If the status is ACTIVE, the decoder manifest can't be edited. If the status is marked DRAFT, you can edit the decoder manifest.

creationTime.description: The time the decoder manifest was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the decoder manifest was last updated in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

message.description: The detailed message for the decoder manifest. When a decoder manifest is in an INVALID status, the message contains detailed reason and help information.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

description.description: A brief description of the fleet.

creationTime.description: The time the fleet was created, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the fleet was last updated in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

status.description: The state of the vehicle model. If the status is ACTIVE, the vehicle model can't be edited. If the status is DRAFT, you can edit the vehicle model.

creationTime.description: The time the vehicle model was created, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the vehicle model was last updated, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the signal catalog was last updated in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

creationTime.description: The time the state template was created, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the state template was last updated, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

vehicleSummaries.description: A list of vehicles and information about them.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the vehicle.

modelManifestArn.description: The ARN of a vehicle model (model manifest) associated with the vehicle.

decoderManifestArn.description: The ARN of a decoder manifest associated with the vehicle.

creationTime.description: The time the vehicle was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the vehicle was last updated in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

attributes.description:
Static information about a vehicle in a key-value pair. For example:

"engineType" : "1.3 L R2"

registerAccountStatus.description: The status of registering your Amazon Web Services account, IAM role, and Timestream resources.

timestreamResources.description: The registered Amazon Timestream resources that Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise edge agent software can transfer your vehicle data to.

iamResources.description: The registered IAM resource that allows Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise to send data to Amazon Timestream.

creationTime.description: The time the account was registered, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time this registration was last updated, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

timestreamDatabaseName.description: The name of the registered Amazon Timestream database.

timestreamTableName.description: The name of the registered Amazon Timestream database table.

vehicles.description: A list of information about a batch of created vehicles. For more information, see the API data type.

errors.description: A list of information about creation errors, or an empty list if there aren't any errors.

vehicleName.description: The unique ID of the vehicle to create.

arn.description: The ARN of the created vehicle.

thingArn.description: The ARN of a created or validated Amazon Web Services IoT thing.

vehicles.description:
A list of information about the batch of updated vehicles.

This list contains only unique IDs for the vehicles that were updated.

errors.description: A list of information about errors returned while updating a batch of vehicles, or, if there aren't any errors, an empty list.

vehicleName.description: The unique ID of the updated vehicle.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the updated vehicle.
The ARN of the created campaign.

name.description: The name of the created decoder manifest.

arn.description: The ARN of the created decoder manifest.

id.description: The ID of the created fleet.

name.description: The name of the created vehicle model.

The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the state template.

id.description: The unique ID of the state template.

vehicleName.description: The unique ID of the created vehicle.

arn.description: The ARN of the created vehicle.

thingArn.description: The ARN of a created or validated Amazon Web Services IoT thing.

arn.description:
The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the deleted campaign.

The ARN isn’t returned if a campaign doesn’t exist.

name.description: The name of the deleted decoder manifest.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the deleted decoder manifest.
The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the deleted fleet.

name.description: The name of the deleted model manifest.

name.description: The name of the deleted signal catalog.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the deleted signal catalog.
The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the state template.

id.description: The unique ID of the state template.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the deleted vehicle.
The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the campaign.

description.description: The description of the campaign.

signalCatalogArn.description: The ARN of a signal catalog.

targetArn.description: The ARN of the vehicle or the fleet targeted by the campaign.

status.description: The state of the campaign. The status can be one of: CREATING, WAITING_FOR_APPROVAL, RUNNING, and SUSPENDED.

startTime.description: The time, in milliseconds, to deliver a campaign after it was approved.

expiryTime.description: The time the campaign expires, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time). Vehicle data won't be collected after the campaign expires.

postTriggerCollectionDuration.description: How long (in seconds) to collect raw data after a triggering event initiates the collection.

diagnosticsMode.description: Option for a vehicle to send diagnostic trouble codes to Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise.

spoolingMode.description: Whether to store collected data after a vehicle lost a connection with the cloud. After a connection is re-established, the data is automatically forwarded to Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise.

compression.description: Whether to compress signals before transmitting data to Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise. If OFF is specified, the signals aren't compressed. If it's not specified, SNAPPY is used.

priority.description: A number indicating the priority of one campaign over another campaign for a certain vehicle or fleet. A campaign with the lowest value is deployed to vehicles before any other campaigns.

signalsToCollect.description: Information about a list of signals to collect data on.

collectionScheme.description: Information about the data collection scheme associated with the campaign.

dataExtraDimensions.description: A list of vehicle attributes associated with the campaign.

creationTime.description: The time the campaign was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The last time the campaign was modified.

dataDestinationConfigs.description:
The destination where the campaign sends data. You can send data to an MQTT topic, or store it in Amazon S3 or Amazon Timestream.

MQTT is the publish/subscribe messaging protocol used by Amazon Web Services IoT to communicate with your devices.

Amazon S3 optimizes the cost of data storage and provides additional mechanisms to use vehicle data, such as data lakes, centralized data storage, data processing pipelines, and analytics.

You can use Amazon Timestream to access and analyze time series data, and Timestream to query vehicle data so that you can identify trends and patterns.

dataPartitions.description: The data partitions associated with the signals collected from the vehicle.

signalsToFetch.description: Information about a list of signals to fetch data from.

maxSampleCount.description: The maximum number of samples to collect.

minimumSamplingIntervalMs.description:
The minimum duration of time (in milliseconds) between two triggering events to collect data.

If a signal changes often, you might want to collect data at a slower rate.

dataPartitionId.description:
The ID of the data partition this signal is associated with.

The ID must match one of the IDs provided in dataPartitions. This is accomplished either by specifying a particular data partition ID or by using default for an established default partition. You can establish a default partition in the DataPartition data type.

If you upload a signal as a condition for a campaign's data partition, the same signal must be included in signalsToCollect.

Access to certain Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise features is currently gated. For more information, see Amazon Web Services Region and feature availability in the Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise Developer Guide.

name.description: The name of the decoder manifest.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the decoder manifest.

description.description: A brief description of the decoder manifest.

modelManifestArn.description: The ARN of a vehicle model (model manifest) associated with the decoder manifest.

status.description: The state of the decoder manifest. If the status is ACTIVE, the decoder manifest can't be edited. If the status is marked DRAFT, you can edit the decoder manifest.

creationTime.description: The time the decoder manifest was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the decoder manifest was last updated in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

message.description: The detailed message for the decoder manifest. When a decoder manifest is in an INVALID status, the message contains detailed reason and help information.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the fleet.

description.description: A brief description of the fleet.

signalCatalogArn.description: The ARN of a signal catalog associated with the fleet.

creationTime.description: The time the fleet was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the fleet was last updated, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

name.description: The name of the vehicle model.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the vehicle model.

description.description: A brief description of the vehicle model.

signalCatalogArn.description: The ARN of the signal catalog associated with the vehicle model.

status.description: The state of the vehicle model. If the status is ACTIVE, the vehicle model can't be edited. You can edit the vehicle model if the status is marked DRAFT.

creationTime.description: The time the vehicle model was created, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The last time the vehicle model was modified.

name.description: The name of the signal catalog.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the signal catalog.

description.description: A brief description of the signal catalog.

nodeCounts.description: The total number of network nodes specified in a signal catalog.

creationTime.description: The time the signal catalog was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The last time the signal catalog was modified.

totalNodes.description: The total number of nodes in a vehicle network.

totalBranches.description: The total number of nodes in a vehicle network that represent branches.

totalSensors.description: The total number of nodes in a vehicle network that represent sensors.

totalAttributes.description: The total number of nodes in a vehicle network that represent attributes.

totalActuators.description: The total number of nodes in a vehicle network that represent actuators.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the state template.

description.description: A brief description of the state template.

signalCatalogArn.description: The ARN of the signal catalog associated with the state template.

stateTemplateProperties.description: A list of signals from which data is collected. The state template properties contain the fully qualified names of the signals.

dataExtraDimensions.description:
A list of vehicle attributes associated with the payload published on the state template's MQTT topic.

Default: An empty array

metadataExtraDimensions.description:
A list of vehicle attributes to associate with user properties of the messages published on the state template's MQTT topic.

Default: An empty array

creationTime.description: The time the state template was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the state template was last updated in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

id.description: The unique ID of the state template.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the vehicle to retrieve information about.

modelManifestArn.description: The ARN of a vehicle model (model manifest) associated with the vehicle.

decoderManifestArn.description: The ARN of a decoder manifest associated with the vehicle.

attributes.description:
Static information about a vehicle in a key-value pair. For example:

"engineType" : "1.3 L R2"

creationTime.description: The time the vehicle was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the vehicle was last updated in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

identifier.description: The unique ID of the state template.

stateTemplateUpdateStrategy.description:
The update strategy for the state template. Vehicles associated with the state template can stream telemetry data with either an onChange or periodic update strategy.

Access to certain Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise features is currently gated. For more information, see Amazon Web Services Region and feature availability in the Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise Developer Guide.

periodic.description: Vehicles associated with the state template will stream telemetry data during a specified time period.

stateTemplateUpdateRate.description: The length of time between state template updates.

onChange.description: Vehicles associated with the state template will stream telemetry data when there is a change.

campaigns.description: Lists information about the state of the vehicle with deployed campaigns.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

status.description:
The status of a campaign, which can be one of the following:

  • CREATED - The campaign exists but is not yet approved.

  • READY - The campaign is approved but has not been deployed to the vehicle. Data has not arrived at the vehicle yet.

  • HEALTHY - The campaign is deployed to the vehicle.

  • SUSPENDED - The campaign is suspended and data collection is paused.

  • DELETING - The campaign is being removed from the vehicle.

  • READY_FOR_CHECKIN - The campaign is approved and waiting for vehicle check-in before deployment.

name.description: The name of the imported decoder manifest.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the decoder manifest that was imported.

name.description: The name of the imported signal catalog.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the imported signal catalog.

networkInterfaces.description: A list of information about network interfaces.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

type.description: The network protocol for the vehicle. For example, CAN_SIGNAL specifies a protocol that defines how data is communicated between electronic control units (ECUs). OBD_SIGNAL specifies a protocol that defines how self-diagnostic data is communicated between ECUs.

canInterface.description: Information about a network interface specified by the Controller Area Network (CAN) protocol.

protocolName.description: The name of the communication protocol for the interface.

protocolVersion.description: The version of the communication protocol for the interface.

obdInterface.description: Information about a network interface specified by the on-board diagnostic (OBD) II protocol.

requestMessageId.description: The ID of the message requesting vehicle data.

pidRequestIntervalSeconds.description: The maximum number message requests per second.

dtcRequestIntervalSeconds.description: The maximum number message requests per diagnostic trouble code per second.

useExtendedIds.description: Whether to use extended IDs in the message.

hasTransmissionEcu.description: Whether the vehicle has a transmission control module (TCM).

vehicleMiddleware.description: The vehicle middleware defined as a type of network interface. Examples of vehicle middleware include ROS2 and SOME/IP.

protocolName.description: The protocol name of the vehicle middleware.

signalDecoders.description: Information about a list of signals to decode.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

fullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of a signal decoder as defined in a vehicle model.

type.description: The network protocol for the vehicle. For example, CAN_SIGNAL specifies a protocol that defines how data is communicated between electronic control units (ECUs). OBD_SIGNAL specifies a protocol that defines how self-diagnostic data is communicated between ECUs.

interfaceId.description: The ID of a network interface that specifies what network protocol a vehicle follows.

canSignal.description: Information about signal decoder using the Controller Area Network (CAN) protocol.

isBigEndian.description: Whether the byte ordering of a CAN message is big-endian.

isSigned.description: Determines whether the message is signed (true) or not (false). If it's signed, the message can represent both positive and negative numbers. The isSigned parameter only applies to the INTEGER raw signal type, and it doesn't affect the FLOATING_POINT raw signal type.

startBit.description:
Indicates the beginning of the CAN signal. This should always be the least significant bit (LSB).

This value might be different from the value in a DBC file. For little endian signals, startBit is the same value as in the DBC file. For big endian signals in a DBC file, the start bit is the most significant bit (MSB). You will have to calculate the LSB instead and pass it as the startBit.

offset.description: The offset used to calculate the signal value. Combined with factor, the calculation is value = raw_value * factor + offset.

factor.description: A multiplier used to decode the CAN message.

length.description: How many bytes of data are in the message.

signalValueType.description: The value type of the signal. The default value is INTEGER.

obdSignal.description: Information about signal decoder using the on-board diagnostic (OBD) II protocol.

serviceMode.description: The mode of operation (diagnostic service) in a message.

pid.description: The diagnostic code used to request data from a vehicle for this signal.

scaling.description: A multiplier used to decode the message.

offset.description: The offset used to calculate the signal value. Combined with scaling, the calculation is value = raw_value * scaling + offset.

bitRightShift.description: The number of positions to shift bits in the message.

bitMaskLength.description: The number of bits to mask in a message.

isSigned.description: Determines whether the message is signed (true) or not (false). If it's signed, the message can represent both positive and negative numbers. The isSigned parameter only applies to the INTEGER raw signal type, and it doesn't affect the FLOATING_POINT raw signal type. The default value is false.

signalValueType.description: The value type of the signal. The default value is INTEGER.

messageSignal.description: The decoding information for a specific message which supports higher order data types.

topicName.description: The topic name for the message signal. It corresponds to topics in ROS 2.

structuredMessage.description: The structured message for the message signal. It can be defined with either a primitiveMessageDefinition, structuredMessageListDefinition, or structuredMessageDefinition recursively.

primitiveMessageDefinition.description: Represents a primitive type node of the complex data structure.

ros2PrimitiveMessageDefinition.description: Information about a PrimitiveMessage using a ROS 2 compliant primitive type message of the complex data structure.

structuredMessageListDefinition.description: Represents a list type node of the complex data structure.

name.description: The name of the structured message list definition.

memberType.description: The member type of the structured message list definition.

listType.description: The type of list of the structured message list definition.

capacity.description: The capacity of the structured message list definition when the list type is FIXED_CAPACITY or DYNAMIC_BOUNDED_CAPACITY.

structuredMessageDefinition.description: Represents a struct type node of the complex data structure.

customDecodingSignal.description:
Information about a custom signal decoder.

Access to certain Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise features is currently gated. For more information, see Amazon Web Services Region and feature availability in the Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise Developer Guide.

fleets.description: A list of fleet IDs that the vehicle is associated with.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

nodes.description: A list of information about nodes.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

branch.description:
Information about a node specified as a branch.

A group of signals that are defined in a hierarchical structure.

fullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the branch. For example, the fully qualified name of a branch might be Vehicle.Body.Engine.

description.description: A brief description of the branch.

deprecationMessage.description: The deprecation message for the node or the branch that was moved or deleted.

comment.description: A comment in addition to the description.

sensor.description:
An input component that reports the environmental condition of a vehicle.

You can collect data about fluid levels, temperatures, vibrations, or battery voltage from sensors.

fullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the sensor. For example, the fully qualified name of a sensor might be Vehicle.Body.Engine.Battery.

dataType.description: The specified data type of the sensor.

description.description: A brief description of a sensor.

unit.description: The scientific unit of measurement for data collected by the sensor.

allowedValues.description: A list of possible values a sensor can take.

min.description: The specified possible minimum value of the sensor.

max.description: The specified possible maximum value of the sensor.

deprecationMessage.description: The deprecation message for the node or the branch that was moved or deleted.

comment.description: A comment in addition to the description.

structFullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the struct node for a sensor if the data type of the actuator is Struct or StructArray. For example, the struct fully qualified name of a sensor might be Vehicle.ADAS.CameraStruct.

actuator.description:
Information about a node specified as an actuator.

An actuator is a digital representation of a vehicle device.

fullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the actuator. For example, the fully qualified name of an actuator might be Vehicle.Front.Left.Door.Lock.

dataType.description: The specified data type of the actuator.

description.description: A brief description of the actuator.

allowedValues.description: A list of possible values an actuator can take.

min.description: The specified possible minimum value of an actuator.

max.description: The specified possible maximum value of an actuator.

deprecationMessage.description: The deprecation message for the node or the branch that was moved or deleted.

comment.description: A comment in addition to the description.

structFullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the struct node for the actuator if the data type of the actuator is Struct or StructArray. For example, the struct fully qualified name of an actuator might be Vehicle.Door.LockStruct.

attribute.description:
Information about a node specified as an attribute.

An attribute represents static information about a vehicle.

fullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the attribute. For example, the fully qualified name of an attribute might be Vehicle.Body.Engine.Type.

dataType.description: The specified data type of the attribute.

description.description: A brief description of the attribute.

allowedValues.description: A list of possible values an attribute can be assigned.

min.description: The specified possible minimum value of the attribute.

max.description: The specified possible maximum value of the attribute.

deprecationMessage.description: The deprecation message for the node or the branch that was moved or deleted.

comment.description: A comment in addition to the description.

struct.description: Represents a complex or higher-order data structure.

fullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the custom structure. For example, the fully qualified name of a custom structure might be ComplexDataTypes.VehicleDataTypes.SVMCamera.

description.description: A brief description of the custom structure.

deprecationMessage.description: The deprecation message for the node or the branch that was moved or deleted.

comment.description: A comment in addition to the description.

property.description: Represents a member of the complex data structure. The datatype of the property can be either primitive or another struct.

fullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the custom property. For example, the fully qualified name of a custom property might be ComplexDataTypes.VehicleDataTypes.SVMCamera.FPS.

dataType.description: The data type for the custom property.

dataEncoding.description: Indicates whether the property is binary data.

description.description: A brief description of the custom property.

deprecationMessage.description: The deprecation message for the node or the branch that was moved or deleted.

comment.description: A comment in addition to the description.

structFullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the struct node for the custom property if the data type of the custom property is Struct or StructArray.

nodes.description: A list of information about nodes.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

branch.description:
Information about a node specified as a branch.

A group of signals that are defined in a hierarchical structure.

fullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the branch. For example, the fully qualified name of a branch might be Vehicle.Body.Engine.

description.description: A brief description of the branch.

deprecationMessage.description: The deprecation message for the node or the branch that was moved or deleted.

comment.description: A comment in addition to the description.

An input component that reports the environmental condition of a vehicle.

You can collect data about fluid levels, temperatures, vibrations, or battery voltage from sensors.

fullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the sensor. For example, the fully qualified name of a sensor might be Vehicle.Body.Engine.Battery.

dataType.description: The specified data type of the sensor.

description.description: A brief description of a sensor.

unit.description: The scientific unit of measurement for data collected by the sensor.

allowedValues.description: A list of possible values a sensor can take.

min.description: The specified possible minimum value of the sensor.

max.description: The specified possible maximum value of the sensor.

deprecationMessage.description: The deprecation message for the node or the branch that was moved or deleted.

comment.description: A comment in addition to the description.

structFullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the struct node for a sensor if the data type of the actuator is Struct or StructArray. For example, the struct fully qualified name of a sensor might be Vehicle.ADAS.CameraStruct.

actuator.description:
Information about a node specified as an actuator.

An actuator is a digital representation of a vehicle device.

fullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the actuator. For example, the fully qualified name of an actuator might be Vehicle.Front.Left.Door.Lock.

dataType.description: The specified data type of the actuator.

description.description: A brief description of the actuator.

allowedValues.description: A list of possible values an actuator can take.

min.description: The specified possible minimum value of an actuator.

max.description: The specified possible maximum value of an actuator.

deprecationMessage.description: The deprecation message for the node or the branch that was moved or deleted.

comment.description: A comment in addition to the description.

structFullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the struct node for the actuator if the data type of the actuator is Struct or StructArray. For example, the struct fully qualified name of an actuator might be Vehicle.Door.LockStruct.

attribute.description:
Information about a node specified as an attribute.

An attribute represents static information about a vehicle.

fullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the attribute. For example, the fully qualified name of an attribute might be Vehicle.Body.Engine.Type.

dataType.description: The specified data type of the attribute.

description.description: A brief description of the attribute.

allowedValues.description: A list of possible values an attribute can be assigned.

min.description: The specified possible minimum value of the attribute.

max.description: The specified possible maximum value of the attribute.

deprecationMessage.description: The deprecation message for the node or the branch that was moved or deleted.

comment.description: A comment in addition to the description.

struct.description: Represents a complex or higher-order data structure.

fullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the custom structure. For example, the fully qualified name of a custom structure might be ComplexDataTypes.VehicleDataTypes.SVMCamera.

description.description: A brief description of the custom structure.

deprecationMessage.description: The deprecation message for the node or the branch that was moved or deleted.

comment.description: A comment in addition to the description.

property.description: Represents a member of the complex data structure. The datatype of the property can be either primitive or another struct.

fullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the custom property. For example, the fully qualified name of a custom property might be ComplexDataTypes.VehicleDataTypes.SVMCamera.FPS.

dataType.description: The data type for the custom property.

dataEncoding.description: Indicates whether the property is binary data.

description.description: A brief description of the custom property.

deprecationMessage.description: The deprecation message for the node or the branch that was moved or deleted.

comment.description: A comment in addition to the description.

structFullyQualifiedName.description: The fully qualified name of the struct node for the custom property if the data type of the custom property is Struct or StructArray.

Tags.description: The list of tags assigned to the resource.

vehicles.description: A list of vehicles associated with the fleet.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

kmsKeyId.description: The ID of the KMS key that is used for encryption.

encryptionType.description: The type of encryption. Set to KMS_BASED_ENCRYPTION to use an KMS key that you own and manage. Set to FLEETWISE_DEFAULT_ENCRYPTION to use an Amazon Web Services managed key that is owned by the Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise service account.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the campaign.

status.description:
The state of a campaign. The status can be one of:

  • CREATING - Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise is processing your request to create the campaign.

  • WAITING_FOR_APPROVAL - After you create a campaign, it enters this state. Use the API operation to approve the campaign for deployment to the target vehicle or fleet.

  • RUNNING - The campaign is active.

  • SUSPENDED - The campaign is suspended. To resume the campaign, use the API operation.

name.description: The name of the updated decoder manifest.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the updated decoder manifest.
The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the updated fleet.

name.description: The name of the updated vehicle model.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the updated vehicle model.

name.description: The name of the updated signal catalog.

arn.description: The ARN of the updated signal catalog.
The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the state template.

id.description: The unique ID of the state template.

arn.description: The ARN of the updated vehicle.
